verdict_rationale: The structural design of 16c is sound — per-tab filter state, AND composition, derived chip lists from visible-tab plugins, and the synthetic-entry as a sibling outside the plugin list are all correct calls. Three concrete blockers must be resolved before landing: the shortcuts regression (two real shortcuts silently deleted, one invented), the empty-state visibility bug under active filters, and the missing vocabulary parity gate. One Playwright smoke covering the canonical demo path is the recommended fourth fix. None require architectural rework; all are targeted changes within the scope of existing tasks.

consumed_by: all composer views that expose the catalog button

dependency_chain.ripple_notes: 16c cannot land before 16b (auditCharacteristics.ts, DataTrustTier, rewritten PluginCard all required). The only outgoing coupling is ChatInput's window event listener — already tested by InlineChatSourceEntry unit tests. The PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT constant living on PluginCard.tsx is an architectural smell (a leaf component that no longer dispatches the event owns the export two consumers depend on), but both 16b and 16c explicitly accepted this; it is not a 16c blocker.

findings.title: ShortcutsHelp rewrite silently deletes two live shortcuts and adds one non-existent one
findings.description: The plan's ACTION_SHORTCUTS array at 16c:1017-1025 drops 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G' (Open graph view) and 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y' (Export YAML). Both are currently documented in ShortcutsHelp.tsx:11-12 and are concretely wired in App.tsx:154-173 (confirmed: Ctrl+Shift+G dispatches OPEN_GRAPH_MODAL_EVENT; Ctrl+Shift+Y dispatches OPEN_YAML_MODAL_EVENT). CommandPalette.tsx:145,156 also surfaces these shortcuts. The plan adds 'Alt+1-4 — Switch inspector tab' to ACTION_SHORTCUTS. No Alt key handler exists anywhere in the frontend codebase (grep of altKey, 'Alt+[1-4]', and all inspector-tab components returns zero results). The ShortcutsHelp component will ship two undocumented real shortcuts and one documented ghost shortcut. On demo day, the operator presses '?' and sees a reference that contradicts the keyboard behavior they just demonstrated.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/App.tsx:154-173 — Ctrl+Shift+G and Ctrl+Shift+Y handlers confirmed present and wired
src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/common/CommandPalette.tsx:145,156 — second documentation site for same shortcuts
findings.required_fix: Restore 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G — Open graph view' and 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y — Export YAML' to the Actions section (or Reference; either is defensible). Delete 'Alt+1-4 — Switch inspector tab' unless the shortcut is implemented in the same commit. Cross-check the final array against App.tsx handlers before merging.
findings.systems_pattern: Stringly typed documentation — shortcut labels are free text that drift from implementation; no mechanical gate ties them together.

findings.title: Synthetic entry is not cordoned from empty-state logic; filters that eliminate all real plugins leave the drawer in an ambiguous state
findings.description: The plan renders InlineChatSourceEntry as a sibling of the plugin list outside the pluginList array. The existing empty-state guard in CatalogDrawer.tsx:340-345 fires when pluginList.length === 0. When a user activates a filter that matches zero real source plugins, the drawer enters the empty-state branch and renders only the 'No plugins matching...' message — the synthetic entry is inside the same conditional as the plugin list rendering (16c:866-872 places InlineChatSourceEntry inside the same render path that is only reached when pluginList.length > 0). On the other reading (the synthetic entry is rendered unconditionally as a sibling above the empty-state node), the user sees the synthetic entry as the apparent 'result' of their filter query, breaking the mental model of filters. The plan's text at 16c:873-875 says the synthetic entry is 'unaffected by filters or search — it's always the first source row, by design' but the implementation sketch nests it inside the same list container without branching around the empty-state guard.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog/CatalogDrawer.tsx:340-364 — existing empty-state guard fires on pluginList.length === 0
findings.required_fix: Render InlineChatSourceEntry unconditionally when activeTab === 'sources', at a point in the JSX tree that is outside the empty-state conditional. The empty-state message and the synthetic entry must both be visible simultaneously when filters match zero real plugins. A unit test should assert: given [csv, azure_blob] filtered down to zero by an active chip, the Sources tab still shows InlineChatSourceEntry and also shows the 'No plugins matching' cue.
findings.systems_pattern: Category violation leaking across the clamped boundary — the plan says the entry is independent but the structural implementation does not enforce that independence.

findings.title: No mechanical parity gate between frontend auditCharacteristics vocabulary and backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS
findings.description: The closed 13-member VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS vocabulary lives in the 16a backend (Python frozenset). Its frontend mirror is 16b's auditCharacteristics.ts metadata table (one object per flag). 16c's FilterChipStrip.tsx:348 calls lookupAuditCharacteristic(flag) and falls back to raw flag display if the lookup misses. This graceful fallback means a 14th characteristic added to 16a without updating 16b's metadata table will silently surface as an unstyled, unlabelled chip using the internal flag string (e.g., 'io_write') rather than a human label (e.g., 'File write'). The test fixtures in 16c:614-628 hard-code 'external_call' and 'io_read'; a rename in 16a passes all frontend tests while breaking the production filter label. There is no test or build step that asserts the two vocabularies are the same set.
evidence: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:344-360 — FilterChipStrip renders audit chips via lookupAuditCharacteristic with fallback to raw flag
16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:614-628 — test fixtures hard-code specific flag strings
16a-phase-7a-backend.md (scope section) — VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS is a 13-member closed frozenset in Python
findings.required_fix: Add a Vitest test that imports the frontend auditCharacteristics metadata table and asserts that the key set matches a locally-duplicated copy of VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (or, better, generate auditCharacteristics.ts from the backend source via a codegen step). Minimal acceptable fix: a test file that fails to compile if a new entry is added to one side but not the other, or a runtime assertion in the test suite that `Object.keys(AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS_TABLE).sort()` equals the expected 13-element sorted array.
findings.systems_pattern: Dual source of truth without a parity gate — a classic Stringly Typed / Implicit Dependency pattern that breaks on the first backend extension.

findings.title: No Playwright E2E gate covers the catalog filter or synthetic entry; staging smoke is manual only
findings.description: The project has a functioning Playwright harness with multiple E2E specs (smoke.spec.ts, composer-guided.spec.ts, composer-proposals.spec.ts, etc.). Phase 1B and Phase 2C both landed Playwright tests. 16c's Task 5 regression gate is Vitest-only plus a manual staging smoke. The canonical demo path — operator opens catalog with Ctrl+Shift+P, clicks 'Try it' on the InlineChatSourceEntry, observes chat input prefilled, activates a filter chip, observes narrowing — is not covered by any automated test. A regression in any of those steps would go undetected until demo day. Per project_rc5ux_demo_prep_scope, the merge bar is 'demo succeeds.'
evidence: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:1103-1176 — Task 5 contains only Vitest + manual smoke, no Playwright spec
src/elspeth/web/frontend/tests/e2e/ — existing Playwright specs confirm harness is alive and maintained
findings.required_fix: Add a Playwright spec (catalog-drawer-phase7.spec.ts or extend an existing spec) covering: (1) Ctrl+Shift+P opens the drawer; (2) InlineChatSourceEntry is visible on Sources tab; (3) 'Try it' closes the drawer and prefills the chat input; (4) a capability-tag chip filters the plugin list. These four assertions cover the load-bearing demo path and would catch the S-002 empty-state bug as a regression under filter activation.
findings.systems_pattern: Drift to Low Performance — each phase that skips automated coverage reduces demo reliability; the cumulative gap is what fails on demo day.

findings.title: Per-tab filtered counts make stale filters invisible on tab badges
findings.description: The counts memo (16c:880-889) applies filtersByTab[tab] to each tab's count so the tab badge shows the filtered count for every tab, not just the active one. A user who activates 'Tier 3' on Sources (leaving 2/6 matching), switches to Transforms, and forgets about the filter sees 'Sources: 2' in the badge. There is no visual indicator that the Sources tab has an active filter while the user is on Transforms. This is a discoverability gap: the user now believes only 2 sources exist, not that they left a filter on.
evidence: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:880-889 — counts memo applies per-tab filtersByTab to all three tab counts

16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:1199-1200 — risk table acknowledges cross-tab filter surprise but only addresses the 'hides every plugin on the other tab' form of the problem, not the badge discoverability form
findings.required_fix: Either: (a) append a dot/indicator to each tab badge when that tab has any active filter (low cost), or (b) only apply the current tab's filter to the current tab badge and show unfiltered counts on inactive tabs. Option (a) is one CSS class + a boolean derived from filtersByTab[tab]. The plan should at minimum acknowledge this discoverability gap and document the chosen design; silence implies the author didn't consider it.
findings.systems_pattern: Balancing loop disruption — the per-tab filter state was introduced to prevent one UX trap (cross-tab filter leakage) and in doing so created a new one (filter-state invisibility on inactive tabs).

findings.title: scorePlugin extension has no canonical-demo regression pin
findings.description: 16c extends scorePlugin (16c:779-793) to fuzzy-match across usage_when_to_use, usage_when_not_to_use, and capability_tags in addition to name and description. Extending the target string changes the fuzzy scores of every plugin against every query. The canonical demo phrase — 'create a list of 5 government web pages and use an LLM to rate how cool they are' — needs web_scrape and an LLM transform to surface. No test pins those queries to the expected first-result plugin. The staging smoke at Task 5 Step 4.9 only types 'URL' and checks for any hit. If a prose extension to an unrelated plugin causes web_scrape to drop below MIN_FUZZY_CONFIDENCE for the demo query, no automated test catches it before demo day.
evidence: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:1164-1165 — Task 5 smoke only types 'URL' without asserting a specific first result
project_composer_canonical_test_case (memory) — canonical demo query is 'government web pages... LLM... rate how cool they are'
findings.required_fix: Add a Vitest parametric test that calls scorePlugin with the canonical demo query fragments ('government', 'web pages', 'LLM') against a representative fixture set including web_scrape and llm transform summaries. Assert that web_scrape and llm score above -1. This is a regression pin, not a ranking oracle; it only needs to detect 'plugin dropped off the search results entirely.'
findings.systems_pattern: Drift to Low Performance — ranking functions degrade quietly because tests observe presence/absence at current state, not invariants across future extensions.

findings.title: FilterChipStrip chip groups have no role=group / aria-labelledby semantics
findings.description: ChipGroup (16c:374-381) renders as a <div> with a label <span>. Chips within the group have aria-pressed (correct). Screen readers will encounter a run of buttons without group boundary semantics, making it impossible to determine which category a chip belongs to without reading all surrounding text. The groups' <span> labels are not associated to the groups via aria-labelledby.
evidence: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:374-381 — ChipGroup renders as div + span with no ARIA role
16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:382-394 — Chip has aria-pressed but no aria-describedby linking to group label
findings.required_fix: Add role='group' and aria-labelledby={groupLabelId} to the ChipGroup <div>; add id={groupLabelId} to the label <span>. One-line addition per group instance.
findings.systems_pattern: Not a system-level risk; localized accessibility gap.

loops_identified.name: Filter-state invisibility on inactive tabs
loops_identified.description: Per-tab filter state prevents cross-tab bleed (good) but creates stale-filter invisibility on badge counts (bad). Each improvement to one form of filter surprise creates the other form.
loops_identified.mitigation_in_plan: No — the risk table acknowledges only the cross-tab bleed form. S-005 above covers the badge form.

loops_identified.name: Vocabulary divergence between 16a and 16b
loops_identified.description: The 16c graceful fallback on unknown audit characteristics means backend vocabulary additions show up as degraded UI without a build failure. The signal (wrong label on a chip) appears in production well after the backend change, far from the cause.
loops_identified.mitigation_in_plan: No mechanical gate. S-003 covers this.

assumptions.assumption: 16b (auditCharacteristics.ts, DataTrustTier, rewritten PluginCard) is merged before 16c lands
assumptions.risk: If both ship as a stacked PR and 16b has a regression in auditCharacteristics.ts, 16c imports a broken module. Plan acknowledges this at 16c:1203 and specifies sequence verification.

assumptions.assumption: CSS pass for new class names lands before Task 5 staging smoke
assumptions.risk: 16c introduces inline-chat-source-entry, filter-chip, shortcuts-subheading, and related classes without CSS. The plan requires a CSS commit 'before Task 5.' If this commit is skipped, the staging smoke reviews unstyled components.
assumptions.severity: acknowledged — plan text is explicit at 16c:66-75

pattern_matches: Dual source of truth (audit characteristics vocabulary)
Drift to Low Performance (ranking + E2E gap)
Shifting the Burden (manual smoke substituting for automated gate)
